title: rubus leucanthus
intro: Rubus leucanthus, also known as the White-berried Bramble, is a type of flowering shrub in the rose family, native to Eastern Asia and parts of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to the presence of anthraquinones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus leucanthus include its ability to regulate bowel movements, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. Herbal preparations of Rubus leucanthus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
